rulings.Case Title: RONALD ANTHONY YATES VS. KATHRYN R. DEVINCENZI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Report Of Referee, Approval Of Referee Fees And Costs, And Discharge Of Referee
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 22, 2023, line 1. 1-OTHER AMY HARRINGTON's Motion For Final Report Of Referee, Approval Of Referee Fees And Costs, And Discharge Of Referee. GRANTED. The Referee has made the required accounting. No opposition filed. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BAYVIEW PRESERVATION, L.P. VS. CHANDRA HALL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 22, 2023, line 4. DEFENDANT LYDELL BENJAMIN's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. OFF CALENDAR. Counsel did not comply with the March 15th Order Shortening Time which stated that service needed to occur on that date electronically to opposing counsel, and personally on the client. All service occurred on March 17th, and the client was served by mail according to the Proofs of Service. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: DEVENDRA PATEL VS. PABLO ANTONIO SOBERANIS MEDINA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina And Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo To Respond To Written Discovery, Produce Documents, And To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 22, 2023, line 6. PLAINTIFF DEVENDRA PATEL's Motion To Compel Defendants Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina And Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo To Respond To Written Discovery, Produce Documents, And To Deem Matters Admitted. 1. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina to Respond to Written Discovery is GRANTED subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories-General Defendant Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina, Set One; Form Interrogatories-Unlawful General Defendant Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina, Set One; and Special Interrogatories to Defendant Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina, Set One; within 5 days of notice of entry of order. 2. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina to Respond to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents is GRANTED subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Requests for Production of Documents to Defendant Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina, Set One; within 5 days of notice of entry of order. 3. Plaintiff's Motion to Deem Each Matter of Defendant Pablo Antonio Soberanis Medina's Requests for Admission Admitted is GRANTED, subject to opposition. 4. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo to Respond to Written Discovery is GRANTED subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories-General Defendant Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo, Set One; Form Interrogatories-Unlawful General Defendant Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo, Set One; and Special Interrogatories to Defendant Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo, Set One; within 5 days of notice of entry of order. 5. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo to Respond to Plaintiff's Request for Production of Documents is GRANTED subject to opposition. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Requests for Production of Documents to Defendant Sierra Mena Rudy Alfredo, Set One; within 5 days of notice of entry of order. 6. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant Sierra Mena Rudy 's Requests for Admission Admitted is GRANTED subject to opposition. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MURAT BAJRAMI VS. CAMERON G. SCOTT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, March 22, 2023, line 8. DEFENDANT CAMERON SCOTT's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. OFF CALENDAR. The Demurrer was filed on March 7, 2023-11 court days prior to the hearing-in violation of CCP 1005(b) which requires the Demurrer to be filed no less than 16 court days prior to the hearing on it. The Proof of Service provides that service occurred by mail on March 14, 2023-6 court days before the hearing-in violation of CCP 1013(c). Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. = (501/CFH)
